creator: mmokhi@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 169244
Patch that automates detection of (base | ports) SSL & removes WITHOUT_SSL

Hi and sorry if i came back to you with delay ;)
After days of talking with MySQL-devs (and ensuring they don't have WITH_SSL=no anymore).
I understood MariaDB doesn't have WITH_SSL=no anymore.
It seems letting user choose SSL library "DURING BUILD TIME" is not safe and stable.
So i used to automate choosing SSL library "DURING BUILD TIME" it goes on principle of base-ssl over ports-ssl over bundled-ssl.

Note that (at least in MySQL) you can still change SSL-related run-time options using switches (like --ssl-mode=DISABLED for disabling SSL, etc).

Be sure if anything changed with this approach (i think things won't change in near future, at least), I'll adapt MySQL with'em ;)

QA -> Portlint : Okay
QA -> Poudriere : 10.X 11.X Okay

Best wishes, Mokhi.
